diagrams.title: Bayes' Theorem Formula
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the Bayes' theorem formula that is the foundation of the Naive Bayes classifier, showing posterior probability, likelihood, prior probability, and evidence
diagrams.purpose: Explains the core mathematical formula P(C|X) = P(X|C) * P(C) / P(X) which calculates the probability of class C given features X

diagrams.title: Feature Independence Assumption
diagrams.description: Diagram illustrating the 'naive' assumption that features are conditionally independent given the class, contrasting with actual feature dependencies
elements: Independent arrows from C to each F
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the key 'naive' assumption that P(X1,X2,X3|C) = P(X1|C) * P(X2|C) * P(X3|C), simplifying calculations

diagrams.title: Classification Pipeline
diagrams.description: Flow diagram showing the step-by-step process of how a Naive Bayes classifier makes predictions from input features to class prediction
diagrams.purpose: Shows the complete classification workflow: computing priors, likelihoods, combining them, and selecting the class with highest posterior probability
